mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects expanding aggregate demand, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDPC1 growth months are not statistically higher than zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate compress equity valuations by raising the discount rate for future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days when the monthly rate hike exceeds 25 basis points.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises >0.25pp in a month.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy supply-side momentum, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon during supply-constrained expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows no predictive power for 60-day forward SPX returns when industrial capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer confidence and credit conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when monthly starts drop by more than 10% over a quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of quarters where HOUST declines >10% over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and freight activity (RSAFS) combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicate overheating demand that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and high CPIAUCSL does not correlate with negative 60-day SPX returns in walk-forward testing (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month increases in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) erode real purchasing power and compress equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as inflation expectations anchor higher.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes show zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple decades of data.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, measured by the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.5 percentage points over two months, signals impending economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE increases >0.5pp over a two-month window.
